questions.question: Choose save cadence and commit behaviour. Default and recommended choices are marked in the option descriptions.

options.label: Save: Every Milestone
options.description: Default and recommended. Update at decisions, milestones, and session breaks.

options.label: Save: Every 5 Messages
options.description: More frequent updates with higher token cost.

options.label: Commit: Auto-commit
options.description: Default and recommended. Commit every update batch automatically.

questions.question: Choose models and dispatch strategy for maintain and read-only PMM work.

options.label: Maintain Model: Haiku
options.description: Default and recommended. Fast and cheap for mechanical edits.

options.label: Maintain Model: Sonnet
options.description: Balanced option for more nuanced updates.

options.label: Readonly Model: Haiku
options.description: Default and recommended for query, recall, status, dump, and viz.

options.label: Strategy: Single
options.description: Default and recommended. Lowest dispatch overhead.

options.label: Strategy: Tiered
options.description: Higher parallelism for larger installs; more expensive.

questions.question: Configure startup loading, recall behavior, and timeline/summary window sizing.

options.label: Session Start: Lazy
options.description: Default and recommended. Avoid extra startup dispatch when memory is already injected.

options.label: Recall Beyond Window: Prompt
options.description: Default and recommended. Ask before searching git history.

options.label: Recall Beyond Window: Auto
options.description: Silently search git history when needed.

options.label: Window: Heavy (100/20)
options.description: Load more recent history on startup.

options.label: Window: Unlimited
options.description: Load full windowed files on startup.

questions.question: Choose how visible PMM update reporting should be.
Select which PMM files stay active. Core files are usually the best default set.

options.label: timeline.md
options.description: Recommended chronology; usually best with a tail strategy.

questions.question: Choose how load strategies should be handled for active Tier 1 files. Use manual mode only when you want per-file overrides.

options.label: Configure Manually
options.description: Follow up with per-file values such as full, head:N, tail:N, header, or skip.
